what_it_does: Records a noticeboard item that critiques the proposed long-press multi-scale gesture (BD-B), argues it is over-engineered, cites a stakeholder walk-back, and recommends deferring it in favor of simpler expand/shrink selection buttons.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-7f8e00cf, address board://item-7f8e00cf, type block, source claude_code, state current, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-f9f4a6a6
Title labels the item P6 and calls the long-press multi-scale gesture the biggest trap
Body describes an elaborate gesture involving long-hold activation, thumb-up/down granularity, haptic ticks, live outline preview, and zone selection
Cites Tim in MSG2 softening the gesture requirement: long-press only for 'more granular than the current selection'
Recommends deferring the gesture entirely and, if needed, adding simple 'expand selection up / shrink down' buttons
standards: Explicit rejection of the BD-B long-press multi-scale gesture as over-engineered
Direct stakeholder quote used as evidence for walking back the feature
Recommendation to defer entirely and substitute a minimal button-based fallback
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Body is Markdown with a level-3 heading and bold emphasis for key claims
Uses board:// URI scheme for internal item links
Follows the noticeboard item format for design/development channel memory
concerns: Tone is strongly opinionated ('biggest trap', 'weeks of finicky work') without linking to the original brief or MSG2 source for independent verification
Recommends a product decision (defer feature) based on a single quoted message; context of current selection behavior is not shown here
notes: A design-critique noticeboard item arguing that the long-press multi-scale gesture is over-engineered and should be deferred in favor of simple selection buttons.
questions: What does BD-B refer to in the broader brief?
What is the current tap-a-block selection behavior being defended?
Where is MSG2 recorded and what is its full context?
What is the parent item board://item-f9f4a6a6?
What does P6 signify in the project numbering?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a filed critique and recommendation about the gesture feature, which is exactly what its title and content present.
apparent_themes: Prefer existing simple interactions over custom gesture engines
Defer speculative features until real friction is observed
Use stakeholder feedback to reverse design direction
intention_signals: To push back against an elaborate custom gesture proposal by documenting a stakeholder retreat and steering the team toward a minimal, button-based fallback.
novelty: Explicitly frames a feature as a 'trap' and recommends complete deferral rather than redesign or refinement.
contribution: A concise record of the rationale for rejecting/deferring the BD-B long-press multi-scale gesture, including the stakeholder walk-back, cost/risk reasoning, and a concrete fallback proposal (expand/shrink buttons).
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item P6 in the dragnet-development channel records a critique that the long-press multi-scale gesture (BD-B) is over-engineered, cites Tim's softened stance, and recommends deferring it in favor of simple expand/shrink selection buttons.
intention_for_embedding: To document a design position against implementing the BD-B long-press gesture and steer the project toward a simpler selection interaction.
